sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.35 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finite group of order$n$. Is it true that for every factorization$n=a_1 a_k$there exist subsets$A_1, ,A_k$such that$|A_1|=a_1, ,|A_k|=a_k$and$G=A_1 A_k$? { M.\,H.\,Hooshmand} No, it is not true. A counterexample with$k=3$is given by the alternating group on 4 letters$G={A}_4$and$(a_1, a_2, a_3) = (2,3,2)$(G.\,M.\,Bergman, Letter of 19 December 2019, ).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
